dialogos.texto_en: Oh?\nI think Itsuki just went into that store right there.
You're right.\nIs that%p-2;――%p; a glass accessory shop over there?
Hey, Futaro.\nShouldn't we check it out too?
Sure, come on.
Uesugi-kun, Nino.\nLooks like both of you took a liking\nto this shop too.
Yes, we could see the beautiful blue color from afar.
As expected.\nThe accessories here are lovely.\nIt's like they've captured the sea and sky of Okinawa.
Yes, that's for sure.\nI heard that they are all handmade,\nso each one has a different shade.
(They all look like the same to me...\n But now that it's been pointed out,\n the colors are indeed different.)
These three here,\nthey represent the sea, the blue sky, and the night sky,\nright? They're all blue, but have a completely different shade.
Woah, this one has green in it, too!
It's probably the emerald green sea\nand the blue sky. The gradation is beautiful.
Yes, it expresses the beauty of Okinawa\nfrom the manufacturer's perspective.
Look at this necklace.\nYou can see stars inside.
So, it might be the night sky?\nAlthough the blue seems a bit too light for that.
It could be representing star sand from the beach.
In that case, it's the sea, huh.
What do you think?\nIt could be the sea, or the starry sky.\nOh, maybe there are starry skies in the sea too.
Teehee!\nThis is really nice.\nYou can come up with many stories from just this one piece.
Hey Futaro, does it look good on me?
Y-yeah, it does, doesn't it?
Huh?\nYou could contribute a bit more to the conversation, you know?
Well, what else should I say?
Maybe you could say that the other one looks better,\nor try to broaden the conversation a bit?
Ggh...\nWell, I guess...
But, it is Futaro we're talking about.
I know that you think it looks good on me.\nThank you.
(That doesn't look like a thank you face.)
(Oh no, I missed the chance of leading her.\n I need to do something to redeem myself...)
Hey, Nino.\nCan you show me that too?
Futaro, you took a liking to that piece too?
Yeah, a little.
(Jeez, it's so expensive!\n It's priced higher than I thought!\n I wanted to buy it for her, but...)
Uesugi-kun?\nIs something wrong? You suddenly froze.
Ah...\nNah, something just struck me as off...
Something struck you as off?
I see.\nIt's fine, Futaro, don't worry about it.
It's not impossible.\nTh-this, isn't a problem at all!
It's fine. You don't have to put on a show.\nI don't want to burden you, Futaro.
Ah...\nSo that's what you meant.
Sorry about that.
Your feelings are more than enough.\nThank you, Futaro.
(I made things awkward.\n I meant to lead, but this is making things worse...)
(At this rate,\n I won't be able to show Nino\n what kind of man I am...)
Sorry...\nThe price is higher than I expected and I don't have enough\nmoney with me.
Oh, that's what you meant.
You don't have to apologize.\nI never asked you to buy it for me, Futaro.
Ahh...
(I'm so useless!\n I can't even buy Nino a present that she likes, can I.)
(In addition, the fact they're kind of helping me out\n like this. This is bad, if I don't do something%p-2;――%p;)
(At this rate,\n I won't be able to show Nino\n what kind of man I am...)
It does suit you after all.
EH?!\nWh-what now, all of a sudden?
Ah, uh, sorry, it just slipped out.\nI just thought it would look good on you\nand wanted to buy it for you...sorry.
It's totally fine.\nYour feelings are more than enough.
You didn't just say that\nwithout thinking, did you.
I'm sorry for doubting you.
Oh, no, it's fine.\nDon't apologize.
But, wow, Nino.\nYou understand Uesugi-kun's true feelings.
Of course.\nFutaro and I are dating, after all.
(I made things awkward for Nino.\n I meant to lead, but this is making things worse...)
(At this rate,\n I won't be able to show Nino\n what kind of man I am...)
